codeReview.summary: Reviewed the original large-MR degradation feature together with the follow-up fixes for degradation-summary persistence and benchmark evidence. The original commit does deterministically choose truncation versus degradation and restricts partial coverage to highest-priority files; the persistence fix now stores the degradation summary on writer-visible run fields and the benchmark evidence follow-up adds a checked-in benchmark corpus, harness, and runtime evidence covering VAL-PERF-001.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The original worker followed the go-reliability procedure, but the benchmark/runtime evidence portion of this feature had to be completed in a separate follow-up feature, which suggests the skill guidance could more strongly emphasize that large-MR reliability work is not complete until checked-in performance evidence exists when the feature contract calls for it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The beta-large-mr-degradation handoff for session e4c99ae4-091a-40d2-b343-bf2cfe57302d explicitly recorded VAL-PERF-001 as undone, while features.json later added and completed beta-large-mr-benchmark-evidence to supply `internal/bench/bench_test.go` and `testdata/bench/runtime_evidence.json`.

summary: Review passed on rerun-after-fixes. I reviewed the prior failed review, the original degradation commit, the degradation-summary persistence fix, the benchmark-evidence follow-up, the relevant transcript skeletons/hand-offs, and the current code. Degradation summaries are now persisted via `review_runs.error_code/error_detail` and rendered end-to-end by the writer, while `internal/bench/bench_test.go` plus `testdata/bench/runtime_evidence.json` provide the requested benchmark corpus and runtime evidence path for VAL-PERF-001.
